title: A Psalm of David, which he sang to the Lord because of the words of Chusi the son of Benjamin.

verses.t: O Lord my God, in thee have I trusted: save me from all them that persecute me, and deliver me.
Lest at any time the enemy seize my soul as a lion, while there is none to ransom, nor to save.
O Lord my God, if I have done this; if there is unrighteousness in my hands;
if I have requited with evil those that requited me with evil: may I then fall away empty before mine enemies.
Let the enemy persecute my soul, and take it; and let him tread my life down to the earth, and lay mine honour in the dust.
Arise, O Lord, in thy wrath; be exalted in the utmost boundaries of mine enemies: awake, O Lord my God, according to the decree which thou didst command.
And the congregation of the nations shall compass thee: and for this cause do thou return on high.
The Lord shall judge the nations: judge me, O Lord, according to my righteousness, and according to mine innocence that is in me.
Oh let the wickedness of sinners come to an end; and thou shalt direct the righteous, O God that searchest the hearts and reins.
My help is righteous, coming from God who saves the upright in heart.
God is a righteous judge, and strong and patient, not bringing forth his anger every day.
If ye will not repent, he will furbish his sword; he has bent his bow, and made it ready.
And on it he has fitted the instruments of death; he has made his arrows for them that burn.
Behold, he has travailed with unrighteousness, he has conceived trouble, and brought forth iniquity.
He has opened a pit and dug it up, and he shall fall into the ditch which he has made.
His trouble shall return upon his own head, and his unrighteousness shall come down upon his own crown.
I will give thanks to the Lord according to his righteousness; I will sing to the name of the Lord most high.
